summary: Frontend-only testing of ATLAS Nettoyage Multiservice one-page static site. All 25+ critical flows passed: branding (ATLAS title/og tags, no Emergent badge/text), header navigation with smooth scroll, hero with 'Excellence' highlighted lime, stats (4), services (6 cards), reasons (6), process (4 steps), gallery (8 images), zones (20 quartiers), FAQ accordion (8 items, expand/collapse OK), contact form (mailto:atlasmultiservicenettoyage@gmail.com with success toast confirmed via UI), WhatsApp link to wa.me/15144760700, footer with both phones + Montréal + copyright, floating actions (scroll-top works), and mobile hamburger menu (375px).
critical_code_review_comments: og:image still hosted on customer-assets.emergentagent.com CDN (https://customer-assets.emergentagent.com/job_instant-web-builder-19/artifacts/jz2x8r9e_image.png). The URL string contains 'emergentagent' but the content displayed is an ATLAS visual. If user wants zero 'emergent' string in any meta value, this asset should be re-hosted on a non-emergent domain. Currently acceptable since requirement focuses on text content ('ATLAS' present, no 'emergent' branding text shown to users).
Contact.jsx form sets submitting=true but only resets after 600ms timeout; if user has no mail client installed nothing else happens. Consider showing a fallback mailto link copy-to-clipboard option.
No client-side email format validation beyond HTML5 'type=email'; minor robustness improvement only.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Site is 100% static React (no backend, no API calls). Public URL: https://instant-web-builder-19.preview.emergentagent.com. All key elements have data-testid attributes (see review_request). Mailto navigation cannot be intercepted via Object.defineProperty on Location.prototype.href in Chromium; verify via success toast 'Votre client mail s'ouvre.' which only fires after window.location.href=mailto:... executes (Contact.jsx:50-56). FAQ accordion uses shadcn AccordionTrigger which sets data-state='open'/'closed' on the inner <button>, not on the AccordionItem div.
